methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.0B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Waymo, a pre-IPO autonomous vehicle company in the AI & Software sector, is evaluated against a peer set of three same-sector comparables spanning public and private stages. The comp universe includes one public company (Palantir at $400.0B) and eight private peers, with the three primary anchors being Palantir, Figure AI ($39.0B, Series C), and Scale AI ($29.0B, private).

Peer-median valuation, the applicable triangulation method, yields $39.0B based on the three-company median. This method reflects a range from $29.0B (Scale AI floor) to $400.0B (Palantir ceiling), with significant dispersion driven by Palantir's public-market premium and stage heterogeneity. The peer-median approach is suitable given sector alignment and comparable operational scale within the autonomous vehicle and AI infrastructure domain.

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $33.1B (low) to $44.9B (high), centered on $39.0B. The current stated valuation of $126B substantially exceeds this implied range, creating a material valuation gap. Key uncertainties include: the representativeness of a single-method analysis with limited peer depth; the non-comparable stage mix (public versus early-stage private); and the absence of revenue, cash burn, or path-to-profitability metrics that would refine the estimate. Further analysis using revenue multiple or DCF methods would be required to reconcile the observed premium or validate the current ask.
